top_weaknesses.issue: Four paragraphs over 80 words, including a 111-word wall ('The 2027 stack a serious user runs...') — major mobile fail and the densest section is the most concrete one
top_weaknesses.fix: Split the 111-word paragraph after 'A persistent memory store backed up like any other file.'; split each 80+ paragraph at its natural pivot

top_weaknesses.issue: Hand-wavy quantitative claims (70B on $2K workstation, 'within roughly a year,' 'bill drops by an order of magnitude,' 'switching cost: zero') will get pounced on by HN
top_weaknesses.fix: Either quantify with one named config/benchmark or downgrade to softer hedges with a footnote

top_weaknesses.issue: 'One model trained to do everything is, by construction, average at most things' is technically sloppy — frontier generalists often beat small specialists without task-specific data
top_weaknesses.fix: Reframe to 'when you have task-specific data and evals, specialists win on cost/latency/quality' — keeps thesis, drops the false universal

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice flips between economics analyst and manifesto ('She is a firm. It is a peer.'); solo lawyer/clinic examples drift to techno-libertarian without addressing liability, eval, compliance
top_weaknesses.fix: Cut one of the three examples and add one concrete workflow or benchmark instead

top_weaknesses.issue: Search-language gap: H2s lack discoverable phrases like 'local AI models,' 'open-weight AI,' 'AI model orchestration,' 'private AI stack'
top_weaknesses.fix: Weave one query phrase into an H2 or early body

consensus_summary: Sticky frame ('constellation vs oracle') with a quotable line ('Your subscription is the amortization schedule'); H2s tell a clean story. Held back by four long paragraphs (one 111-word wall), unsubstantiated quantitative claims, and a manifesto closer that drifts off the analyst voice.
